sn20.5:1.2: “Monks, suppose there were a sharp-bladed spear,
sn20.5:1.3: and a man were to come along saying,
sn20.5:1.4: ‘With my hand or fist I will bend back this sharp-bladed spear, fold it in two, and roll it up.’
sn20.5:1.6: Would that man be able with his hand or fist to bend back that sharp-bladed spear, fold it in two, and roll it up?”
sn20.5:1.9: Because a sharp-bladed spear isn’t easy to bend back, fold in two, or roll up.
sn20.5:1.10: The man would simply reap his share of trouble & vexation.”
sn20.5:2.1: “In the same way, monks, when a monk’s awareness-release through good-will is cultivated, developed, pursued, given a means of transport, given a grounding, steadied, consolidated, & well-undertaken,
sn20.5:2.2: any non-human being who would think of deranging that monk’s mind would simply reap his share of trouble & vexation.
sn20.5:2.4: ‘Our awareness-release through good-will will be cultivated, developed, pursued, given a means of transport, given a grounding, steadied, consolidated, & well-undertaken.
sn20.5:2.5: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
